title: Allow setting volume and network names in Quadlet
body:
This commit extends `Volume` and `Network` unit definitions with two additional parameters, `VolumeName` and `NetworkName`, which will, respectively, set a user-defined name for the corresponding volume and network. This is similar to how the `ContainerName` directive currently works, and should allow for smoother transitions to Quadlet-managed resources.

Closes: #19003

```release-note
Allow for setting user-defined names for Quadlet `Volume` and `Network` units via the `VolumeName` and `NetworkName` directives, respectively.
```

comments.body:
The reason the test does not pass is that since Quadlet files were processed independently, the test code is designed to handle only a single file. I think you have two options here.
1. Add a new test type (or enhance the current one) that gets a list of quadlet files. Then the test can copy all the files to the source directory, Quadlet will process them all in the same run which will manage the dependency and then the test needs to verify each of the files.
2. In e2e test only independent files and add a system test case in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/system/252-quadlet.bats

I think option 2 is better because we should have a system test for this feature even if we have an e2e one.

> The reason the test does not pass is that since Quadlet files were processed independently, the test code is designed to handle only a single file. I think you have two options here.
> 
>     1. Add a new test type (or enhance the current one) that gets a list of quadlet files. Then the test can copy all the files to the source directory, Quadlet will process them all in the same run which will manage the dependency and then the test needs to verify each of the files.
> 
>     2. In e2e test only independent files and add a system test case in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/system/252-quadlet.bats
> 
> 
> I think option 2 is better because we should have a system test for this feature even if we have an e2e one.

Thanks so much -- I went with option 2 as suggested, and left only self-contained tests for E2E.

While I thought the code is OK, it seems that the tests disagree. I can see that the network name was not parsed changed correctly. This is the `ExecStart` of the service:
```
ExecStart=/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups=split --network=systemd-dep_bZffOqSmGY --sdnotify=conmon -d quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 top
```
You can see that the name of the network is incorrect

> While I thought the code is OK, it seems that the tests disagree. I can see that the network name was not parsed changed correctly. This is the `ExecStart` of the service:

> ```

Indeed -- I'll need to get my tests running locally so I'm not wasting CI/your time with extraneous commits here, but will push fixes and move this out of draft status ASAP. Thanks for keeping an eye out!
Sure, np. Just in case you've missed this one: https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#integration-tests
Fixed tests and moved this to In Review -- I think this should be good to go (feature/bug-wise).
